title: Quadlet does not allow setting apparmor profiles (or unconfined)
body:
### Issue Description

Since podman uses the [`--security-opt=option`](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#security-opt-option) parameter for several separate things, and Quadlet decided not to introduce a single SecurityOpt option but instead multiple options like `SeccompProfile`, `SecurityLabel*`, `Mask`, that all lead to different `--security-opt` parameters, I am not able to set `--security-opt=apparmor=unconfined`, because there just [doesn't seem to be](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#container-units-container) a corresponding Quadlet option for AppArmor.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

write quadlet .container unit with `AppArmor=unconfined`

### Describe the results you received

the generated `podman run` call in the service unit does not contain the parameter `--security-opt=apparmor=unconfined`

### Describe the results you expected

the generated `podman run` call in the service unit should contain the parameter `--security-opt=apparmor=unconfined`

### podman info output

```yaml
not really relevant, the latest documentation does not list the setting either, so it just does not seem to exist in any version.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Contributions welcome to add this field. Note as workaround there is always `PodmanArgs=` which can be used to pass arbitrary cli arguments to the podman command.
I would like to work on this
@tusharrrr1 thanks, I've assigned the issue to you
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I would like to take a stab at this issue if it has gone stale.
